description_long: This train station is a single-story brick building with a symmetrical rectangular layout and a prominent central entrance. The facade features decorative white trim around the windows and doors, as well as a raised sign above the main entrance, which is accessed by a small staircase and ramp. The roof is gabled and covered with reddish-brown tiles, and the building is accented by white cornices and pilasters. The windows are tall and evenly spaced, giving the structure a balanced and formal appearance. The side views reveal the building's depth and the consistent use of brick and white detailing around all exterior openings. There are no visible canopies, tracks, or platform structures attached to the building.
description: A symmetrical, single-story brick train station with a gabled red-tiled roof, white decorative trim, and a central entrance with a sign above. The facade is formal, with tall windows and classic architectural details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, central sign, staircase, ramp, and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the building's depth, gabled roof, and end doorway with white trim.
description_view_2: Rear view, similar to the front but without the entrance sign, showing symmetrical window placement and roof structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with visible end doorway and consistent brickwork.
attribute_station_signage: One prominent sign above the main entrance displaying the station name.
